controls.title: Liability of management bodies
controls.summary: Management bodies of essential and important entities are responsible for approving cybersecurity risk measures and overseeing their implementation. Members can be held personally liable.

controls.title: Cybersecurity risk-management measures
controls.summary: Entities must adopt technical, operational, and organisational measures proportionate to the risk, covering: policies, incident handling, business continuity, supply chain, network security, access control, cryptography, and asset management.

controls.title: Governance
controls.summary: Management bodies must be trained in cybersecurity and promote regular training for employees to acquire sufficient knowledge and skills to identify risks and assess practices.

controls.title: Cybersecurity measures — detailed requirements
controls.summary: Mandatory measures include: incident response plans; business continuity (backup, disaster recovery); supply chain security; secure development practices; vulnerability handling; MFA; encryption; and encrypted communications.

controls.title: Reporting obligations for significant incidents
controls.summary: Significant incidents must be notified to the CSIRT or competent authority within 24 hours (early warning), within 72 hours (incident notification), and with a final report within one month.

controls.title: Use of European cybersecurity certification schemes
controls.summary: Member States may require essential and important entities to use ICT products, services, or processes certified under an EU cybersecurity certification scheme.

controls.title: Jurisdiction
controls.summary: Essential and important entities fall under the jurisdiction of the Member State where they are established; DNS providers and TLD registries follow additional rules.

controls.title: Register of essential and important entities
controls.summary: Entities must register with their national competent authority, providing name, address, contact, sector, and IP address ranges within specified deadlines.

controls.title: Peer reviews
controls.summary: Member States may participate in peer-review exercises of their NIS2 implementation organised by ENISA to learn from best practices and identify gaps.
